steps.title: Find the logical clue
steps.content: The sentence before the blank says the USGS omitted important considerations, especially the earth's curvature, which implies its earlier determination is flawed.

steps.title: Apply the clue to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect: because the USGS omitted important considerations (especially earth's curvature), it follows that the USGS-determined center was inaccurate. The blank needs a transition signaling that logical consequence.
  • Choice A — ‘Next,’: signals simple sequential order in time, so fails — the inaccuracy is a logical conclusion from the omission, not just the next event. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘That being said,’: signals concession or qualification, so fails — the second sentence draws a direct conclusion, not a caveat. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Hence,’: signals a logical conclusion that follows from the preceding evidence, fitting perfectly. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘However,’: signals contrast, so fails — the second sentence is the expected result of the omission, not an opposing idea. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
